assessed_clauses.original_text: From and after the date of this Agreement until the earlier of the Effective Time and the termination of this Agreement pursuant to Article VIII, except (the following exceptions set forth in clauses (i) through (iv), the "Interim Covenant Exceptions") (i) as otherwise required or expressly contemplated by this Agreement, (ii) as may be required by a Governmental Entity or required by applicable Law or Order, (iii) as may be consented to in writing by Parent (which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld, conditioned or delayed), or (iv) as otherwise set forth in Section 6.1(a) of the Company Disclosure Schedule, the Company shall, and shall cause each of its Subsidiaries to, use commercially reasonable efforts to conduct its business in the ordinary course of business and use commercially reasonable efforts to preserve intact its business organization, keep available the services of its and their present officers and key employees, and maintain its existing business relationships and goodwill, including with material customers, suppliers and other business partners; provided, however, that no action or failure to take an action with respect to matters specifically addressed by the provisions of Section 6.1(b) shall constitute a breach of this Section 6.1(a) unless such action or failure to take action would constitute a breach of such provision of Section 6.1(b).

assessed_clauses.original_text: (vi) amend, modify, extend, renew or terminate any material Lease, and shall not enter into any material new lease, sublease, license or other agreement for the use or occupancy of any real property, other than in the ordinary course of business consistent with past practice;

assessed_clauses.covenant_explanation: This covenant restricts the ability to amend, terminate, or enter into material leases or agreements for real property outside of normal business operations.

outlier_analysis.why_unusual: This clause is unusual because it imposes broad restrictions on the ability to engage in any significant real property transactions, including amendments to existing leases and entering into new leases, which is not typical for asset disposition covenants that usually focus on the sale or transfer of assets rather than operational agreements.
specific_differences: The clause restricts not only the disposition of assets but also the operational flexibility regarding leases, which is not commonly seen in typical asset disposition covenants.
It includes a requirement for actions to be consistent with 'past practice,' which adds a subjective element that is not typically present in standard covenants.
The clause encompasses a wide range of agreements related to real property, including subleases and licenses, rather than focusing solely on equity acquisitions or direct asset sales.
outlier_analysis.comparison_to_cluster: Typical asset_dispositions covenants primarily deal with the sale or transfer of assets, while this clause restricts operational activities related to real property, which is a broader and more restrictive approach than what is usually found in the matched cluster.
lawyer_should_review_for: The potential impact on the company's operational flexibility and ability to adapt to market conditions.
The vagueness of the term 'ordinary course of business consistent with past practice' and how it may be interpreted.
The implications of restricting new leases or modifications on the company's growth strategy and real estate portfolio.
unusual_elements: Broad restrictions on both existing and new leases, which is atypical for asset disposition clauses.
The inclusion of subjective language regarding 'ordinary course of business' which can lead to ambiguity.
The comprehensive nature of the restrictions that encompass various types of agreements related to real property.

assessed_clauses.original_text: (vii) purchase any real property or sell any Owned Real Property;
assessed_clauses.processed_text: (vii) purchase any real property or sell any Owned Real Property;
assessed_clauses.cluster_theme: Restrictions on Implementing New Policies and Strategic Operational Changes
red_flags: absolute prohibition on purchasing or selling real property
limits operational flexibility
assessed_clauses.covenant_explanation: This covenant restricts the buyer from purchasing or selling any real property during the interim period.

outlier_analysis.why_unusual: This clause is unusual because it specifically restricts the purchase or sale of real property, which is a narrow focus compared to typical operational covenants that generally address broader strategic operational changes and policy implementations. Most operational covenants allow for some degree of flexibility in property transactions as part of normal business operations.
specific_differences: The clause focuses exclusively on real property transactions, whereas benchmark patterns often encompass a wider range of operational changes including policy adjustments, capital expenditures, and strategic initiatives.

It lacks language regarding the rationale or conditions under which such transactions may be permissible, which is common in typical operational covenants that provide some leeway for necessary business decisions.
The clause does not address the implications of these restrictions on the company's overall operational flexibility, which is a typical consideration in similar covenants.
outlier_analysis.comparison_to_cluster: This clause differs from typical operations_conduct covenants in that it is overly restrictive and specific, focusing solely on real estate transactions without considering the broader operational context. Most covenants in this cluster allow for a range of operational adjustments to adapt to changing market conditions.
lawyer_should_review_for: The potential impact on the company's ability to respond to market opportunities or challenges due to the restriction on real property transactions.
The lack of clarity regarding exceptions or conditions under which real property transactions may be allowed, which could lead to operational difficulties.
The overall alignment of this clause with the company's strategic goals and whether it may hinder necessary business decisions.
unusual_elements: Narrow focus on real property transactions without broader operational context.
Absence of exceptions or conditions for the restrictions imposed.
Potentially excessive limitations on operational flexibility.

assessed_clauses.original_text: (xvii) (A) make (except in the ordinary course of business consistent with past practice), change or revoke any material Tax election, (B) change any material Tax accounting method, (C) file any amended Tax Return, (D) enter into any closing agreement (or any other agreement with any Governmental Entity) with respect to Taxes or any Tax allocation or sharing agreement (other than any commercial agreement entered into in the ordinary course of business consistent with past practice that does not relate primarily to Taxes), (E) settle or pay any income or other material Tax claim, audit, assessment or dispute, (F) extend or waive the application of any statute of limitations regarding the assessment or collection of any Tax (other than with respect to customary automatically granted extensions of time within which to file Tax Returns) or initiate any voluntary disclosure or similar program, (G) file any income or other material Tax Return in a manner inconsistent with past practices or (H) surrender any right to claim a refund of a material amount of Taxes;

assessed_clauses.original_text: (xix) except as required pursuant to the terms of any Company Benefit Plan in effect as of the date of this Agreement, (A) increase in any manner the compensation or consulting fees, bonus, pension, welfare, fringe or other benefits, severance or termination pay of any current or former employee, officer, director or other individual service provider of the Company or its Subsidiaries, (B) become a party to, establish, adopt, amend, modify, commence participation in or terminate any Company Benefit Plan or any plan, policy, program, contract, agreement or arrangement that would have been a Company Benefit Plan had it been entered into prior to the date of this Agreement, (C) grant any new awards, or amend or modify the terms of any outstanding awards, under any Company Benefit Plan or otherwise, (D) take any action to accelerate the vesting or lapsing of restrictions or payment, or fund or in any other way secure the payment of, compensation or benefits under any Company Benefit Plan or otherwise, (E) materially change any actuarial or other assumptions used to calculate funding obligations with respect to any Company Benefit Plan that is required by applicable Law to be funded or change the manner in which contributions to such plans are made or the basis on which such contributions are determined, except as may be required by GAAP or (F) terminate the employment or service of any employee, officer, director or other individual service provider or Third Party Worker of the Company or its Subsidiaries with an annual salary or wage rate or consulting fees in excess of $200,000 other than for cause;

assessed_clauses.original_text: Nothing set forth in this Agreement shall give Parent, directly or indirectly, the right to control or direct the Company's or its Subsidiaries' operations prior to the Effective Time. Prior to the Effective Time, the Company and its Subsidiaries shall exercise, consistent with the terms and conditions of this Agreement, complete control and supervision over their respective operations.

assessed_clauses.cluster_theme: Clarification That Buyer Has No Pre-Closing Control Rights
seller_favorable_aspects: Complete control over operations retained by the Company and its Subsidiaries until the Effective Time
assessed_clauses.covenant_explanation: This covenant restricts the Parent from exercising control over the Company's operations before the merger is finalized.

assessed_clauses.original_text: From and after the execution and delivery of this Agreement, until the earlier of the Effective Time and the termination of this Agreement pursuant to Article VIII, Parent shall not, and shall cause its Subsidiaries and controlled Affiliates not to, directly or indirectly (whether by merger, consolidation or otherwise), acquire, purchase, lease or license or otherwise enter into a transaction with (or agree to acquire, purchase, lease, or license or otherwise enter into a transaction with) any Person if doing so would reasonably be expected to prevent or materially delay the obtaining of any authorization, consent, Order or approval of any Governmental Entity or the expiration or termination of any applicable waiting period necessary to consummate the Transactions.
